current_record: 43-30-9, 95 points (4th in Metropolitan Division, missed playoffs)

venue.name: Capital One Arena
venue.notes: NHL hockey configuration (NBA basketball capacity is 20356). Shared with Washington Wizards; undergoing $800M renovation through 2027

ownership.ownership_group: Monumental Sports & Entertainment (Leonsis has owned the Capitals since 1999)

front_office.notes: Patrick promoted to GM in July 2024; MacLellan moved to President of Hockey Operations and Patrick reports to him

head_coach.name: Spencer Carbery
head_coach.background: Former AHL coach of the year with Hershey Bears; previously assistant with Toronto Maple Leafs. Hired by Washington in May 2023.

coaching_staff.name: Ray Bennett
coaching_staff.notes: Hired May 2026 to replace departed Kirk Muller

roster.name: Alex Ovechkin
roster.notes: Captain; all-time NHL goals leader; led team in goals (32) and points (64) in 2025-26

recent_games.notes: Season finale; Ovechkin had power-play assist on game-winner

sources.notes: Season record, division standing, team leaders
Full roster by position with jersey numbers
Confirms Chris Patrick GM and Brian MacLellan as President of Hockey Operations
Ray Bennett hired as assistant coach

verification_notes: Head coach (Spencer Carbery) confirmed and given 'vote of confidence' by GM in April 2026 per multiple sources. GM (Chris Patrick) and President of Hockey Operations (Brian MacLellan) confirmed via NHL.com and ESPN. Owner (Ted Leonsis / Monumental Sports) confirmed. Coaching staff: Allen, Wellar, McCudden, Murray are returning; Kirk Muller departed after 2025-26; Ray Bennett added May 2026. Record (43-30-9, 95 pts, missed playoffs as 4th in Metro) confirmed via RMNB. Roster pulled directly from ESPN team roster page. Corey Schueneman shares #64 with David Kampf on ESPN page -- omitted from final roster list as duplicate likely reflects mid-season callup. Capacity 20,000 per Monumental Sports description (shared arena with Wizards). Stadium opened 1997 per same source. Upcoming games empty: NHL offseason, no playoff games. Capitals' captain is Ovechkin; alternate captains traditionally Wilson and others.
